notes: No publisher · zine-traded homebrew (1996) · WAITING HORROR. One night, August 14, 1976, the watchman. Made by a survivor's kid on burned EEPROM; cease-and-desisted by Oneironautics' lawyers in 1997 — the paper trail that proves the studio knew the name Nika Voss years before Pirate Summer shipped.

shelf.publisher_locale: traded at Pacific Northwest zine fairs
shelf.hardware: burned EEPROM · hand-labeled · Slowstick-compatible if you ask it nicely
shelf.shelf_condition: hand-cut sleeve · single-ink print · the label is the author's own letterform, two letters mirrored
shelf.prior_owner: a gift from the author, Portland zine fair
shelf.provenance_note: Olaf's provenance note is one line: 'I asked her why. She said: so it's someone's JOB to stay awake.'
shelf.cover_blurb: you are the night watchman. rounds are hourly. the log is the job. nothing happens. keep the log anyway.
back_of_case: · type the log yourself. the log keeps everything.
· the log has kept everything since 1977.
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: The log file persists across every run of the cartridge. Olaf's copy arrives with nineteen years of entries already in it, including the author's. The date is on the log header. The game never mentions it again.
shelf.meta_note: Cease-and-desisted 1997. Oneironautics' lawyers cited 'confusing similarity of geography.' The geography is not similar. It is the same. That is the point.

lore_tokens_conditional.sweetgum_island_light_logged_attempt: you tried to file the light · NOT A STATION
